key_points: Resources are things that satisfy human needs and have value
Resources must be technologically accessible, economically feasible, and culturally acceptable
Resources are classified as natural, human-made, and human resources
Renewable resources can regenerate naturally, while non-renewable resources take millions of years to form
Sustainable development focuses on using resources carefully to protect them for future generations
examples: Natural resources: Water, air, soil, forests, and minerals
Human-made resources: Buildings, roads, bridges, and machines
Human resources: Skilled workers, teachers, engineers
Renewable resources: Solar energy, wind energy, water
Non-renewable resources: Coal, petroleum, natural gas
important_terms: Resource: Anything that satisfies human needs and has value
Natural Resource: Resource obtained directly from nature
Human-made Resource: Resource created by humans using natural materials
Human Resource: People’s knowledge, skills, and abilities
Sustainable Development: Using resources responsibly without harming future generations
exam_tips: Remember the three conditions required for something to become a resource
Understand the classification of resources: natural, human-made, and human
Learn the difference between renewable and non-renewable resources
Remember examples of each type of resource
Understand the importance of sustainable development and resource conservation
